analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet commemorates Soviet WWII sniper Lyudmila Pavlichenko, praising her record and noting her visits to the UK, Canada, and the US and friendship with Eleanor Roosevelt. Evaluation: RU1 (West immoral/hostile) – Not promoted; the tweet mentions positive ties with Western figures and contains no criticism of the West. RU2 (Russia leads an alternative system) – Not addressed; no mention of systems or multilateralism. RU3 (Russia is a benevolent partner) – Not the focus; while it notes friendly ties with Eleanor Roosevelt, the tweet is biographical, not portraying Russia’s partnership role. RU4 (Russian history is admirable) – Promoted; it valorizes a Soviet WWII heroine, highlighting Soviet contribution and historical achievement. RU5 (Russia at the world’s forefront) – Not applicable; no current successes referenced. RU6 (Russia interesting culturally/naturally) – Not applicable; this is not about culture/travel/heritage tourism. Conclusion: RU4 fits.
